articles.title: Extended Kalshi Code OREGONLIVE1: New $10 Masters bonus in WA, CA, and UT
articles.summary: Kalshi is rolling out a new promotional code OREGONLIVE1 that gives users in Washington, California, and Utah a $10 bonus tied to Masters betting. The geographically targeted promo signals Kalshi is aggressively pushing into sports betting territory while testing regional marketing strategies ahead of bigger tournaments.

articles.title: Democratic lawmakers demand oversight on prediction markets and crackdown on wagers on war
articles.summary: Democratic lawmakers are pushing for increased regulation of prediction markets and want to ban betting on war-related events. The regulatory pressure signals growing political heat around the industry just as platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi gain mainstream attention, potentially threatening the current hands-off approach that's fueled their rapid growth.

articles.title: An Eddie Murphy comedy shaped insider trading law for prediction markets
articles.summary: The 1983 film "Trading Places" starring Eddie Murphy accidentally created the legal framework that governs how prediction markets handle insider information today. The movie's plot about commodities manipulation was so realistic that Congress passed the "Eddie Murphy Rule" in 2010, which now determines what kind of non-public information can legally move markets.

articles.title: The big state gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: States are placing massive bets on prediction markets as a revenue source, regulatory framework, or both. If they get it right, we're looking at legitimized mainstream adoption, but if they botch the regulations, it could set the industry back years.

articles.title: House Democrats call on federal regulator to crack down on offshore prediction market war bets
articles.summary: House Democrats are pushing federal regulators to ban or restrict prediction markets that allow betting on wars and military conflicts, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi that operate offshore betting exchanges. The crackdown could signal broader regulatory hostility toward prediction markets, especially those covering sensitive geopolitical events that lawmakers view as inappropriate for wagering.

articles.title: Polymarket Readies Platform Overhaul With New Collateral Token
articles.summary: Polymarket is overhauling its platform with a new collateral token, moving away from its current USDC-only system. This could slash transaction fees and attract more international users who've been priced out by Ethereum gas costs and stablecoin restrictions.

articles.title: Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
articles.summary: Polymarket is launching a major platform overhaul that will give them direct control over both trade execution and outcome resolution instead of relying on third-party infrastructure. This shift could eliminate the friction and delays that have plagued the platform, making it faster and more reliable for high-stakes political betting when every minute counts.

articles.title: New Jersey cannot regulate Kalshi's prediction market, US appeals court rules
articles.summary: A federal appeals court ruled that New Jersey lacks authority to regulate Kalshi's prediction markets, blocking the state's regulatory interference. The decision strengthens prediction markets' legal standing and signals that federal oversight trumps state-level attempts to shut down legitimate betting platforms.

articles.title: Polymarket apologizes after letting users bet on downed U.S. pilots in Iran: 'It should not have been posted'
articles.summary: Polymarket pulled a market that let users bet on whether U.S. pilots would be captured or killed after their aircraft went down in Iranian territory, issuing an apology and calling the market inappropriate. The incident highlights how prediction platforms struggle to balance free market principles with basic human decency, potentially inviting regulatory scrutiny that could limit what markets they can offer going forward.

articles.title: Polymarket apologizes, takes down wager on US pilot’s rescue - Straight Arrow News
articles.summary: Polymarket pulled a betting market on whether a downed US military pilot would be successfully rescued, issuing an apology for the controversial wager. The move highlights how prediction platforms struggle to balance free market principles with public sensitivity around life-or-death situations involving real people.

articles.title: ‘Dystopian death market’: Polymarket halts bets regarding US airman after Moulton condemns site
articles.summary: Polymarket suspended betting markets related to a US airman's death after facing criticism from Representative Seth Moulton, who called the platform a "dystopian death market." The controversy highlights growing regulatory pressure on prediction markets that touch sensitive topics, potentially forcing platforms to narrow their scope to avoid political backlash.
